reference_answer: MARCS-CMS 710327. Stated in the title and header.
Center for Tobacco Products (CTP). Listed in the header "Issuing Office" field and in the body's opening sentence ("The Center for Tobacco Products of the U.S. Food and Drug Administration…").
"To Whom It May Concern:" — there is no named individual addressee in the body, even though the header lists vaping.com / support@vaping.com.
John E. Verbeten, Director, Office of Compliance and Enforcement, Center for Tobacco Products. From the single signature block.
CTPCompliance@fda.hhs.gov. Stated twice — once for the response submission and again as the contact for questions.
RW2502296. Stated in the response-instructions paragraph: "Please note your reference number, RW2502296, in your response…"

In the second long body paragraph beginning "Generally, to be legally marketed in the United States…" — that is, before the bold subheading "**New Tobacco Products Without Required Marketing Authorization Are Adulterated and Misbranded**". The date is then repeated within that subsection's first sentence. So strictly the first occurrence is in the unsubheaded portion above the first bold subheading.
DPAL-WL Response, Office of Compliance and Enforcement FDA Center for Tobacco Products c/o Document Control Center Building 71, Room G335 10903 New Hampshire Avenue Silver Spring, MD 20993-0002

It corresponds to section 201(rr) of the FD&C Act. It defines "tobacco product" to include products that are made or derived from tobacco or contain nicotine from any source and are intended for human consumption. As amended on March 15, 2022, the definition expressly covers products "containing nicotine from any source."

Six separate cc blocks: 1. SV3, LLC d/b/a Vaping.com (Ste 103, McDowell Rd, Phoenix) 2. SV3, LLC (2303 N 44th Street, Unit 2, Phoenix; geoff@smokingvapor.com) 3. SV3, LLC d/b/a Smoking Vapor (2303 N 44th St, Ste 11, Phoenix) — this block contains a **(b)(6)** redaction (FOIA Exemption 6, personal privacy — likely a personal email or phone number) 4. SV3, LLC (8258 W Thunderbird Rd, Peoria, AZ) 5. GoDaddy.com, LLC (abuse@godaddy.com) 6. Shopify, Inc. (abuse@shopify.com)
1. Mixed Fruit Funky Republic Ti7000 2. Tropical Delight Funky Republic Fi3000

- 4908 E McDowell Road, Ste 103, Phoenix, AZ 85008 — SV3, LLC d/b/a Vaping.com - 2303 N 44th Street, Unit 2, Phoenix, AZ 85008 — SV3, LLC (with geoff@smokingvapor.com) - 2303 N 44th St, Ste 11, Phoenix, AZ 85008 — SV3, LLC d/b/a Smoking Vapor - 8258 W Thunderbird Rd, Peoria, AZ 85381 — SV3, LLC (GoDaddy.com, LLC and Shopify, Inc. are listed by email only; no street address.)
Amir Hakak, Geoff Habicht, and Jeannie Day. Amir Hakak and Geoff Habicht appear in four of the cc blocks; Jeannie Day appears only in the SV3, LLC d/b/a Smoking Vapor block.

AZ (Arizona) — appears in the header recipient block (Phoenix) and in all four SV3 cc blocks (Phoenix and Peoria). MD (Maryland) — appears in the FDA response postal address (Silver Spring). So two distinct state codes: AZ and MD.
Letter date: June 6, 2025. "Content current as of": 06/17/2025 (June 17, 2025).
11 calendar days (June 6, 2025 → June 17, 2025).
Within 15 working days from the date of receipt of the letter (not from the date of the letter itself).
February 15, 2007. Any tobacco product not commercially marketed in the U.S. as of that date — or modified after that date — is a "new tobacco product" under section 910(a) of the FD&C Act.
March 15, 2022. The Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2022 (Public Law 117-103, Division P, Title I, Subtitle B).

- Civil money penalties - Seizure - Injunction - Detention or refusal of admission of imported tobacco products The letter explicitly notes that the Warning Letter itself does **not** constitute "written notice" for purposes of section 303(f)(9)(B)(i)(II) of the FD&C Act, which is a qualification on the civil-money-penalty pathway.

- Adulterated under section 902(6)(A) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. § 387b(6)(A)) — because the products lack a marketing authorization order under section 910(c)(1)(A)(i). - Misbranded under section 903(a)(6) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. § 387c(a)(6)) — because a section 905(j) notice was not provided.

The response must include (i) the dates on which the firm discontinued the violative sale and/or distribution of the listed tobacco products, and (ii) the firm's plan for maintaining compliance with the FD&C Act going forward. It also notes that if the firm believes the products are not in violation, it should include its reasoning and any supporting information for FDA's consideration.

The legal entity is SV3, LLC. Per the cc list: - SV3, LLC d/b/a Vaping.com — at 4908 E McDowell Road, Ste 103 - SV3, LLC d/b/a Smoking Vapor — at 2303 N 44th St, Ste 11 So SV3, LLC operates under two relevant d/b/a names: "Vaping.com" and "Smoking Vapor." It also appears under its bare name (SV3, LLC) at additional addresses. The web hosting (GoDaddy.com) and storefront platform (Shopify, Inc.) are cc'd as separate non-affiliated entities at abuse@ addresses, indicating the platforms are being notified but are not themselves the subject of the violations.

The misbranding finding is based on the firm's failure to provide a notice (or other information) under section 905(j) of the FD&C Act (21 U.S.C. § 387e(j)) — a substantial-equivalence report. Section 905(j) is also referenced earlier in the letter as one of the two procedural alternatives to a full premarket marketing authorization order under section 910(c)(1)(A)(i). The same statutory section thus appears in both the "what's required" framing (early in the letter) and the "what's missing" finding (in the adulteration/misbranding paragraph) — joining those two parts of the letter.
